rulings.Case Title: KENNETH GARRETT VS. GWENDOLYN MCCRAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Enforce The Stipulated Settlement Pursuant To Ccp 664.6 For Judgment And Order Compelling Performance Forthwith Declaration Of Amanda L Ebey In Support Of Motion To Enforce Stipulated Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 9, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT GWENDOLYN MCCRAY, DONALD GARRETT Motion To Enforce The Stipulated Settlement Pursuant To Ccp 664.6 For Judgment And Order Compelling Performance Forthwith Declaration Of Amanda L Ebey In Support Of Motion To Enforce Stipulated Settlement Status Report. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HERNAN CARLO MACDULA ET AL VS. SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 9, 2021 line 9. DEFENDANT SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action. Note that as to the cause of action for negligence this Court will follow Alvarez v. BAC Home Loans Servicing, L.P. (2014) 228 Cal.App.4th 941 and to the extent the demurrer is based on the lack of duty argument, it is overruled. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN LIBERTY INVESTMENTS, LP VS. KRISTA ITZHAK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 9, 2021 line 16. DEFENDANT KRISTA ITZHAK DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to clearly allege which tenant protection scheme is applicable to the tenancy at issue in this case. See Complaint paragraph 7 v. Supplemental Allegations paragraph 10(a). Both are not applicable per the terms of the TPA 2019, CC 1946.2(g)(1)(A). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
